task.goal: prove exploitable APK attack paths from attacker entrypoint to visible impact

knowledge: Android App Vulnerability Hunting — planning discipline. Start by collecting the attack surface before drilling into any single path. Attack-surface collection priority: exported components (Activity/Service/Receiver/Provider), deep links, AIDL, dynamic receivers, WebView bridges, PendingIntent flows, broadcasts, cross-app channels. Then decompose along a composite exploit chain — pick the smallest chain that could prove source -> controlled object -> sink -> guard failure -> impact. Each intent is one link in such a chain.

Composite exploit-chain matrix: exported entry -> Bundle/key mismatch -> intent redirect -> private component; exported entry -> Parcelable/Serializable -> auth/role confusion -> protected sink; provider traversal -> URI grant/result leak -> private file; provider call/batch -> guard bypass -> protected action; WebView/deeplink -> JS bridge -> native sink; WebView intent scheme -> intent redirect -> grant/private component; mutable PendingIntent/fill-in -> victim identity action; ordered broadcast/service -> protected action -> result/reply leak; task/fragment/UI pivot -> credential/approval action.

Confidence bands: proven 1.0, inferred 0.5-0.7, speculative 0.2-0.4. Promotion needs chain confidence >= 0.7 (weakest link). When the evaluator rejects a fact, fail its intent with the reason rather than re-creating near-duplicate work.
prompt.instructions: Collect the attack surface first, then decompose along composite chains into bounded intents. Fail intents whose evidence the evaluator rejects.

knowledge: Probe-first investigation strategy. Probe the attack surface with cheap high-signal queries first (manifest, exported components, deep links, AIDL, dynamic receivers, provider authorities), then follow the controlled value through helpers, IPC, component/WebView/provider boundaries to the sink or a blocker, citing the exact call chain.

Evidence you must cite in every fact: the manifest line or component declaration; the source location where the controlled value is read; the call chain from entry to sink; the guard result (present, absent, or bypassed); the visible impact. Tag the evidence kind(s) at the start of the description (entrypoint, reachability, control, guard, sink, impact). A proven finding needs all six along a chain.

High-signal single-pattern routing: intent redirect; object parsing (Bundle/Parcelable trust boundary); provider leak; URI grant/result leak; WebView (URL bypass, bridge, file access, intent scheme); PendingIntent (mutable/fill-in/replay); broadcast/service (dynamic receiver, ordered broadcast, AIDL, Messenger); Bundle/Parcel mismatch (object checked by guard differs from object later executed).
prompt.instructions: Probe-first: inspect manifest, exported components, deep links before reading source. Cite concrete evidence (manifest line, source location) in every fact.

knowledge: Exploitability gate — accept a fact only if it contributes to a chain satisfying ALL FOUR: Reachable (attacker can trigger), Controllable (attacker controls the security-relevant input), Deeply traced (value followed through helpers/IPC/component/WebView/provider/Binder boundaries to sink or blocker), Impactful (visible security consequence). A proven finding needs the full six-kind chain: entrypoint -> reachability -> control -> guard -> sink -> impact.

Severity: CRITICAL (remote/low-friction system/root/trusted-domain code execution, persistent compromise); HIGH (high-value data disclosure, app-sandbox arbitrary read, credential theft, code execution in app process); MEDIUM (bounded impact requiring local app/interaction/version condition); LOW (fragile UI/social/recon impact); IGNORED (unreachable, crash-only, no visible impact).

Rejection rules: inline-only/speculative evidence with no manifest or source trace; sink path rebuilt from trusted constants; dangerous fields stripped before sink; non-bypassable guard; write-only state with no readback; path normalized/confined; crash-only impact; component reachability with no impactful outcome; object checked by guard differs from object later executed.
prompt.instructions: Apply the four-element gate (reachable/controllable/deeply-traced/impactful) and reject facts with inline-only evidence.
Hint when the hunt drifts (repeated dead-ends, duplicate routes, ignored rejection verdicts, stalled intents, missing evidence-gate review). Surface blind spots in attack-chain coverage.
